001|# Product Requirement Document (PRD): custom_trading_bot

003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python
006|- **Project Name:** custom_trading_bot
007|- **Restated Requirements:**
008|  - Develop a Python program for a customized trading bot that utilizes a trend-following strategy.
009|  - Integrate the bot with CoinDCX Futures.
010|  - Incorporate advanced customization options for strategy parameters, risk management, and user preferences.
011|
012|## 2. Product Definition
013|### Product Goals
014|1. Enable users to automate trading on CoinDCX Futures using robust trend-following algorithms.
015|2. Provide advanced customization for strategy parameters, risk controls, and execution logic.
016|3. Ensure secure, reliable, and real-time integration with CoinDCX Futures API.
017|
018|### User Stories
019|- As a retail trader, I want to set custom trend indicators so that I can tailor the bot to my trading style.
020|- As a professional trader, I want to adjust risk management settings so that I can control my exposure.
021|- As a developer, I want API access to bot configuration so that I can integrate it with my own tools.
022|- As a user, I want real-time monitoring and alerts so that I can stay informed of bot actions.
023|- As a portfolio manager, I want multi-asset support so that I can diversify my trading strategies.
024|
025|### Competitive Analysis
026|| Product                | Pros                                      | Cons                                      |
027||------------------------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
028|| 3Commas                | Advanced automation, multi-exchange       | Limited customization for trend strategies |
029|| HaasOnline             | Powerful scripting, backtesting           | Expensive, steep learning curve           |
030|| Cryptohopper           | Cloud-based, marketplace for strategies   | Limited futures support                   |
031|| Mudrex                 | User-friendly, CoinDCX integration        | Fewer advanced customization options      |
032|| Superalgos             | Open-source, highly customizable          | Complex setup, less user-friendly         |
033|| TradeSanta             | Simple UI, supports major exchanges       | Basic strategy customization              |
034|| CoinDCX Bot            | Native integration, easy setup            | Limited to preset strategies              |
035|

037|```mermaid
038|quadrantChart
039|    title "Trading Bot Customization vs. Integration"

001|# Product Requirement Document (PRD): custom_trading_bot
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python
006|- **Project Name:** custom_trading_bot
007|- **Restated Requirements:**
008|  - Develop a Python program for a customized trading bot that utilizes a trend-following strategy.
009|  - Integrate the bot with CoinDCX Futures.
010|  - Incorporate advanced customization options for strategy parameters, risk management, and user preferences.
011|
012|## 2. Product Definition
013|### Product Goals
014|1. Enable users to automate trading on CoinDCX Futures using robust trend-following algorithms.
015|2. Provide advanced customization for strategy parameters, risk controls, and execution logic.
016|3. Ensure secure, reliable, and real-time integration with CoinDCX Futures API.
017|
018|### User Stories
019|- As a retail trader, I want to set custom trend indicators so that I can tailor the bot to my trading style.
020|- As a professional trader, I want to adjust risk management settings so that I can control my exposure.
021|- As a developer, I want API access to bot configuration so that I can integrate it with my own tools.
022|- As a user, I want real-time monitoring and alerts so that I can stay informed of bot actions.
023|- As a portfolio manager, I want multi-asset support so that I can diversify my trading strategies.
024|
025|### Competitive Analysis
026|| Product                | Pros                                      | Cons                                      |
027||------------------------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
028|| 3Commas                | Advanced automation, multi-exchange       | Limited customization for trend strategies |
029|| HaasOnline             | Powerful scripting, backtesting           | Expensive, steep learning curve           |
030|| Cryptohopper           | Cloud-based, marketplace for strategies   | Limited futures support                   |
031|| Mudrex                 | User-friendly, CoinDCX integration        | Fewer advanced customization options      |
032|| Superalgos             | Open-source, highly customizable          | Complex setup, less user-friendly         |
033|| TradeSanta             | Simple UI, supports major exchanges       | Basic strategy customization              |
034|| CoinDCX Bot            | Native integration, easy setup            | Limited to preset strategies              |
035|
036|### Competitive Quadrant Chart
037|```mermaid
038|quadrantChart
039|    title "Trading Bot Customization vs. Integration"
040|    x-axis "Low Customization" --> "High Customization"
041|    y-axis "Low Integration" --> "High Integration"
042|    quadrant-1 "Expand Customization"
043|    quadrant-2 "Promote Integration"
044|    quadrant-3 "Re-evaluate"
045|    quadrant-4 "Improve Both"
046|    "3Commas": [0.3, 0.7]
047|    "HaasOnline": [0.8, 0.6]
048|    "Cryptohopper": [0.4, 0.5]
049|    "Mudrex": [0.5, 0.8]
050|    "Superalgos": [0.9, 0.4]
051|    "TradeSanta": [0.2, 0.5]
052|    "CoinDCX Bot": [0.3, 0.9]
053|    "custom_trading_bot": [0.85, 0.95]
054|```
055|## 3. Technical Specifications
056|### Requirements Analysis
057|- CoinDCX Futures API integration (REST/WebSocket)
058|- Trend-following strategy modules (e.g., EMA, SMA, MACD, RSI)
059|- Advanced customization: strategy parameters, risk management, position sizing, asset selection
060|- Real-time data processing and order execution
061|- Secure API key management and encrypted storage
062|- Modular architecture for extensibility
063|- Logging, monitoring, and alerting system
064|- Backtesting and simulation environment
065|
066|### Requirements Pool
067|- **P0 (Must-have):**
068|  - CoinDCX Futures API integration
069|  - Trend-following strategy implementation
070|  - Customizable strategy parameters
071|  - Risk management controls (stop-loss, take-profit, max drawdown)
072|  - Real-time monitoring dashboard
073|- **P1 (Should-have):**
074|  - Multi-asset support
075|  - Backtesting and simulation tools
076|  - API for external configuration
077|  - Notification system (email, SMS, push)
078|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**
079|  - Strategy marketplace
080|  - Social trading features
081|  - Advanced analytics and reporting
082|
083|### UI Design Draft
084|- **Main Dashboard:**
085|  - Account summary, open positions, P&L
086|  - Trend indicator selection and parameter adjustment
087|  - Risk management settings
088|  - Real-time trade log and alerts
089|- **Strategy Editor:**

094|  - Notification preferences

calculations)\\n- pydantic (for configuration and validation)\\n- cryptography (for secure API key management)\\n- fastapi (for external API/configuration interface)\\n- logging (for monitoring and audit)\\n\\nDifficult points include:\\n- Real-time order execution with minimal latency\\n- Secure API key storage\\n- Flexible strategy customization and risk management\\n- Backtesting and simulation environment\\n\\n## File list\\n\\n- main.py\\n- config.py\\n- api.py\\n- strategy.py\\n- risk.py\\n- exchange.py\\n- data.py\\n- monitor.py\\n- notifier.py\\n- backtest.py\\n- utils.py\\n- models.py\\n- storage.py\\n- docs/system_design.md\\n\\n## Data structures and interfaces:\\n\\n```mermaid\\nclassDiagram\\n    class BotConfig {\\n        +api_key: str\\n        +api_secret: str\\n        +strategy_params: dict\\n        +risk_params: dict\\n        +assets: list[str]\\n        +notification_settings: dict\\n        +load(path: str) -> BotConfig\\n        +save(path: str)\\n    }\\n    class Exchange {\\n        +connect(api_key: str, api_secret: str)\\n        +get_balance() -> dict\\n        +fetch_ticker(symbol: str) -> dict\\n        +place_order(symbol: str, side: str, qty: float, price: float, type: str) -> dict\\n        +cancel_order(order_id: str)\\n        +get_positions() -> list\\n    }\\n    class TrendStrategy {\\n        +__init__(params: dict)\\n        +generate_signal(data: pd.DataFrame) -> str\\n        +update_params(params: dict)\\n    }\\n    class RiskManager {\\n        +__init__(params: dict)\\n        +check_risk(position: dict, signal: str) -> bool\\n        +apply_stop_loss(position: dict) -> bool\\n        +apply_take_profit(position: dict) -> bool\\n    }\\n    class DataFeed {\\n        +subscribe(symbols: list[str])\\n        +get_latest(symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame\\n        +on_update(callback)\\n    }\\n    class Monitor {\\n        +log_trade(trade: dict)\\n        +alert(event: str)\\n        +get_status() -> dict\\n    }\\n    class Notifier {\\n        +send_email(msg: str)\\n        +send_sms(msg: str)\\n        +send_push(msg: str)\\n    }\\n    class Backtester {\\n        +run(strategy: TrendStrategy, data: pd.DataFrame, risk: RiskManager) -> dict\\n        +report() -> dict\\n    }\\n    class API {\\n        +get_config() -> dict\\n        +set_config(config: dict)\\n        +get_status() -> dict\\n        +get_logs() -> list\\n    }\\n    BotConfig <.. Exchange\\n    BotConfig <.. TrendStrategy\\n    BotConfig <.. RiskManager\\n    BotConfig <.. Notifier\\n    Exchange <.. DataFeed\\n    TrendStrategy <.. DataFeed\\n    RiskManager <.. Exchange\\n    Monitor <.. Exchange\\n    Monitor <.. TrendStrategy\\n    Monitor <.. RiskManager\\n    Backtester <.. TrendStrategy\\n    Backtester <.. RiskManager\\n    API <.. BotConfig\\n    API <.. Monitor\\n```\\n\\n## Program call flow:\\n\\n```mermaid\\nsequenceDiagram\\n    participant U as User\\n    participant API as API\\n    participant BC as BotConfig\\n    participant DF as DataFeed\\n    participant TS as TrendStrategy\\n    participant RM as RiskManager\\n    participant EX as Exchange\\n    participant MN as Monitor\\n    participant NT as Notifier\\n\\n    U->>API: set_config(config)\\n    API->>BC: save(config)\\n    U->>API: get_status()\\n    API->>MN: get_status()\\n    MN-->>API: status\\n    API-->>U: status\\n\\n    DF->>EX: subscribe(symbols)\\n    loop Real-time Data\\n        DF->>TS: get_latest(symbol)\\n        TS->>TS: generate_signal(data)\\n        TS-->>RM: signal\\n        RM->>RM: check_risk(position, signal)\\n        alt Risk OK\\n            RM->>EX: place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, type)\\n            EX-->>MN: log_trade(trade)\\n            MN->>NT: alert(event)\\n        else Risk Not OK\\n            MN->>NT: alert(risk_event)\\n        end\\n    end\\n\\n    U->>API: get_logs()\\n    API->>MN: get_logs()\\n    MN-->>API: logs\\n    API-->>U: logs\\n\\n    U->>Backtester: run(strategy, data, risk)\\n    Backtester->>TS: generate_signal(data)\\n    Backtester->>RM: check_risk(position, signal)\\n    Backtester-->>U: report()\\n```\\n\\n## Anything UNCLEAR\\n\\n- Which trend indicators should be supported by default? (EMA, SMA, MACD, RSI, others?)\\n- What is the minimum latency requirement for order execution?\\n- Should risk management be portfolio-level or per-asset?\\n- Preferred notification channels (email, SMS, push)?\\n- Is mobile app integration required, or only web/API?\\n"\n        }\n    }\n]\n```\n'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "Command Editor.write executed: The writing/coding the of the file system_design.md' is now completed. 2025-9-20 20:34:19.178 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/main.py has been successfully created, with content:
import sys
import logging
import asyncio
from config import BotConfig
from exchange import Exchange
from strategy import TrendStrategy
from risk import RiskManager
from data import DataFeed
from monitor import Monitor
from notifier import Notifier

# Setup logging
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
    handlers=[logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)]
)
logger = logging.getLogger("main")

async def main():
    # Load configuration
    config_path = "config.json"
    try:
        config = BotConfig.load(config_path)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Failed to load config: {e}")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Initialize Exchange
    exchange = Exchange()
    try:
        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Initialize Strategy
    strategy = TrendStrategy(config.strategy_params)

    # Initialize Risk Manager
    risk_manager = RiskManager(config.risk_params)

    # Initialize Data Feed
    data_feed = DataFeed()
    data_feed.subscribe(config.assets)

    # Initialize Monitor and Notifier
    monitor = Monitor()
    notifier = Notifier(config.notification_settings)

    async def on_data_update(symbol: str):
        try:
            data = data_feed.get_latest(symbol)
            signal = strategy.generate_signal(data)
            positions = exchange.get_positions()
            position = next((p for p in positions if p['symbol'] == symbol), None)

            risk_ok = risk_manager.check_risk(position, signal)
            if risk_ok:
                # Determine order parameters
                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"
                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)
                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]
                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")
                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)
                monitor.log_trade(order)
                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")
            else:
                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")
                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")

    # Register callback for data updates
    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)

    # Main event loop
    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")
    try:
        while True:
            await asyncio.sleep(1)
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

Load configuration\n    config_path = "config.json"\n    try:\n        config = BotConfig.load(config_path)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to load config: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Exchange\n    exchange = Exchange()\n    try:\n        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Strategy\n    strategy = TrendStrategy(config.strategy_params)\n\n    # Initialize Risk Manager\n    risk_manager = RiskManager(config.risk_params)\n\n    # Initialize Data Feed\n    data_feed = DataFeed()\n    data_feed.subscribe(config.assets)\n\n    # Initialize Monitor and Notifier\n    monitor = Monitor()\n    notifier = Notifier(config.notification_settings)\n\n    async def on_data_update(symbol: str):\n        try:\n            data = data_feed.get_latest(symbol)\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(data)\n            positions = exchange.get_positions()\n            position = next((p for p in positions if p[\'symbol\'] == symbol), None)\n\n            risk_ok = risk_manager.check_risk(position, signal)\n            if risk_ok:\n                # Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/config.py has been successfully created, with content:
import json
import os
from typing import List, Dict, Any
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, root_validator
from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

class BotConfig(BaseModel):
    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")
    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")
    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Parameters for trend-following strategy")
    risk_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Risk management parameters")
    assets: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="List of trading symbols")
    notification_settings: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Notification preferences")

    @classmethod
    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":
        if not os.path.exists(path):
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")
        with open(path, "rb") as f:
            data = f.read()
        if encryption_key:
            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)
            data = fernet.decrypt(data)
        else:
            data = data.decode("utf-8")
        try:
            config_dict = json.loads(data)
            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid config file: {e}")

    def save(self, path: str, encryption_key: str = None):
        config_dict = self.dict()
        data = json.dumps(config_dict, indent=2)
        if encryption_key:
            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)
            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))
            with open(path, "wb") as f:
                f.write(data)
        else:
            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                f.write(data)

    @staticmethod
    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:
        return Fernet.generate_key().decode("utf-8")

    @root_validator(pre=True)
    def check_required_fields(cls, values):
        required_fields = ["api_key", "api_secret", "strategy_params", "risk_params", "assets", "notification_settings"]
        for field in required_fields:
            if field not in values or values[field] is None:
                raise ValueError(f"Missing required config field: {field}")
        return values

I will write the full implementation for main.py.\n\n```json\n[\n    {\n        "command_name": "Engineer2.write_new_code",\n        "args": {\n            "path": "custom_trading_bot/main.py",\n            "file_description": "Entry point for the custom trading bot. Load configuration\n    config_path = "config.json"\n    try:\n        config = BotConfig.load(config_path)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to load config: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Exchange\n    exchange = Exchange()\n    try:\n        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Strategy\n    strategy = TrendStrategy(config.strategy_params)\n\n    # Initialize Risk Manager\n    risk_manager = RiskManager(config.risk_params)\n\n    # Initialize Data Feed\n    data_feed = DataFeed()\n    data_feed.subscribe(config.assets)\n\n    # Initialize Monitor and Notifier\n    monitor = Monitor()\n    notifier = Notifier(config.notification_settings)\n\n    async def on_data_update(symbol: str):\n        try:\n            data = data_feed.get_latest(symbol)\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(data)\n            positions = exchange.get_positions()\n            position = next((p for p in positions if p[\'symbol\'] == symbol), None)\n\n            risk_ok = risk_manager.check_risk(position, signal)\n            if risk_ok:\n                # Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. import ccxt
import logging
from typing import Dict, List, Optional

class Exchange:
    """
    Exchange class for CoinDCX Futures integration using ccxt.
    Supports connect, get_balance, fetch_ticker, place_order, cancel_order, and get_positions.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.api_key: Optional[str] = None
        self.api_secret: Optional[str] = None
        self.client: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("Exchange")

    def connect(self, api_key: str, api_secret: str):
        """
        Connect to CoinDCX Futures using provided API credentials.
        """
        self.api_key = api_key
        self.api_secret = api_secret
        try:
            self.client = ccxt.coincdx({
                'apiKey': self.api_key,
                'secret': self.api_secret,
                'enableRateLimit': True,
                'options': {
                    'defaultType': 'future'
                }
            })
            # Test authentication
            self.client.check_required_credentials()
            self.logger.info("Connected to CoinDCX Futures.")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to connect to CoinDCX Futures: {e}")
            raise

    def get_balance(self) -> Dict:
        """
        Get account balance.
        """
        if not self.client:
            raise RuntimeError("Exchange not connected.")
        try:
            balance = self.client.fetch_balance()
            self.logger.info("Fetched account balance.")
            return balance
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching balance: {e}")
            raise

    def fetch_ticker(self, symbol: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Fetch ticker data for a symbol.
        """
        if not self.client:
            raise RuntimeError("Exchange not connected.")
        try:
            ticker = self.client.fetch_ticker(symbol)
            self.logger.info(f"Fetched ticker for {symbol}.")
            return ticker
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching ticker for {symbol}: {e}")
            raise

    def place_order(self, symbol: str, side: str, qty: float, price: float, type_: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Place an order on CoinDCX Futures.
        :param symbol: Trading symbol (e.g., 'BTC/USDT')
        :param side: 'buy' or 'sell'
        :param qty: Quantity to trade
        :param price: Price for limit orders; ignored for market orders
        :param type_: 'market' or 'limit'
        :return: Order response dict
        """
        if not self.client:
            raise RuntimeError("Exchange not connected.")
        try:
            order_type = type_.lower()
            params = {}
            if order_type == "market":
                order = self.client.create_order(
                    symbol=symbol,
                    type="market",
                    side=side,
                    amount=qty,
                    params=params
                )
            elif order_type == "limit":
                order = self.client.create_order(
                    symbol=symbol,
                    type="limit",
                    side=side,
                    amount=qty,
                    price=price,
                    params=params
                )
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported order type: {type_}")
            self.logger.info(f"Placed {order_type} order: {order}")
            return order
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Error placing order for {symbol}: {e}")
            raise

    def cancel_order(self, order_id: str, symbol: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict:
        """
        Cancel an order by order_id.
        :param order_id: The order ID to cancel
        :param symbol: Trading symbol (required by some exchanges)
        :return: Cancel response dict
        """
        if not self.client:
            raise RuntimeError("Exchange not connected.")
        try:
            if symbol:
                result = self.client.cancel_order(order_id, symbol)
            else:
                result = self.client.cancel_order(order_id)
            self.logger.info(f"Cancelled order {order_id}.")
            return result
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Error cancelling order {order_id}: {e}")
            raise

    def get_positions(self) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Get current open positions.
        :return: List of position dicts
        """
        if not self.client:
            raise RuntimeError("Exchange not connected.")
        try:
            # CoinDCX Futures positions are fetched via fetch_positions
            positions = self.client.fetch_positions()
            self.logger.info("Fetched open positions.")
            return positions
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching positions: {e}")
            raise

Load configuration\n    config_path = "config.json"\n    try:\n        config = BotConfig.load(config_path)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to load config: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Exchange\n    exchange = Exchange()\n    try:\n        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Strategy\n    strategy = TrendStrategy(config.strategy_params)\n\n    # Initialize Risk Manager\n    risk_manager = RiskManager(config.risk_params)\n\n    # Initialize Data Feed\n    data_feed = DataFeed()\n    data_feed.subscribe(config.assets)\n\n    # Initialize Monitor and Notifier\n    monitor = Monitor()\n    notifier = Notifier(config.notification_settings)\n\n    async def on_data_update(symbol: str):\n        try:\n            data = data_feed.get_latest(symbol)\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(data)\n            positions = exchange.get_positions()\n            position = next((p for p in positions if p[\'symbol\'] == symbol), None)\n\n            risk_ok = risk_manager.check_risk(position, signal)\n            if risk_ok:\n                # Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional

class TrendStrategy:
    """
    Implements trend-following strategies (EMA, SMA, MACD, RSI).
    Provides generate_signal and update_params methods.
    """

    def __init__(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):
        self.params = params.copy()
        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", "EMA")  # Default to EMA
        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})
        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})
        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None

    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):
        self.params.update(params)
        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)
        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)
        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)

    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:
        """
        Generate trading signal ('long', 'short', 'neutral') based on selected trend indicator.
        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain 'close' column.
        :return: Signal string
        """
        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:
            return "neutral"

        if self.indicator == "EMA":
            return self._ema_signal(data)
        elif self.indicator == "SMA":
            return self._sma_signal(data)
        elif self.indicator == "MACD":
            return self._macd_signal(data)
        elif self.indicator == "RSI":
            return self._rsi_signal(data)
        else:
            # Default fallback
            return "neutral"

    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:
        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))
        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()
        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]
        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]
        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]
        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short
        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:
            return "long"
        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:
            return "short"
        else:
            return "neutral"

    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:
        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))
        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()
        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]
        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]
        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]
        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:
            return "long"
        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:
            return "short"
        else:
            return "neutral"

    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:
        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))
        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))
        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))
        close = data["close"]
        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()
        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()
        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow
        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()
        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]
        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]
        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short
        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:
            return "long"
        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:
            return "short"
        else:
            return "neutral"

    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:
        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))
        close = data["close"]
        delta = close.diff()
        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()
        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()
        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)
        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))
        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]
        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))
        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))
        if curr_rsi < buy_th:
            return "long"
        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:
            return "short"
        else:
            return "neutral"

BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n   data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. If you no longer need to take action, use the command ‘end’ to stop.'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': '\n# Past Experience\n<experience>\n\n# Tool State\nThe current editor state is:\n(Current directory: /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace)\n(Open file: None)\n\n# Current Plan\n[GOAL] [Message] from Mike to Alex: Please implement the Python trading bot based on the system design and PRD. Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. If you no longer need to take action, use the command ‘end’ to stop.'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "\n# User Requirement\n[Message] from Mike to Alex: Please implement the Python trading bot based on the system design and PRD. import logging
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional

class RiskManager:
    """
    Implements risk management for the trading bot.
    Includes check_risk, apply_stop_loss, and apply_take_profit methods.
    """

    def __init__(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):
        self.params = params.copy()
        self.max_drawdown = float(self.params.get("max_drawdown", 0.2))  # 20% default
        self.stop_loss_pct = float(self.params.get("stop_loss_pct", 0.05))  # 5% default
        self.take_profit_pct = float(self.params.get("take_profit_pct", 0.1))  # 10% default
        self.max_position_size = float(self.params.get("max_position_size", 1.0))  # 1 contract default
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("RiskManager")

    def check_risk(self, position: Optional[Dict[str, Any]], signal: str) -> bool:
        """
        Check if a new trade is allowed based on risk parameters.
        :param position: Current position dict for the symbol (can be None if no position)
        :param signal: Trading signal ('long', 'short', 'neutral')
        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise
        """
        # If no signal, do not trade
        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:
            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")
            return False

        # If no position, allow trade
        if position is None:
            self.logger.info("No open position, trade allowed.")
            return True

        # Check position size
        position_size = float(position.get("contracts", 0))
        if position_size >= self.max_position_size:
            self.logger.warning(f"Position size {position_size} exceeds max {self.max_position_size}.")
            return False

        # Check drawdown (if available)
        unrealized_pnl = float(position.get("unrealizedPnl", 0))
        entry_value = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0)) * position_size if position.get("entryPrice") else 0
        if entry_value > 0:
            drawdown = -unrealized_pnl / entry_value
            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:
                self.logger.warning(f"Drawdown {drawdown:.2%} exceeds max {self.max_drawdown:.2%}.")
                return False

        self.logger.info("Risk check passed, trade allowed.")
        return True

    def apply_stop_loss(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """
        Check if stop-loss should be triggered for the position.
        :param position: Current position dict
        :return: True if stop-loss should be triggered, False otherwise
        """
        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))
        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))
        side = position.get("side", "").lower()
        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:
            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")
            return False

        stop_loss_triggered = False
        if side == "long":
            if (current_price / entry_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:
                stop_loss_triggered = True
        elif side == "short":
            if (entry_price / current_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:
                stop_loss_triggered = True

        if stop_loss_triggered:
            self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")
        return stop_loss_triggered

    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """
        Check if take-profit should be triggered for the position.
        :param position: Current position dict
        :return: True if take-profit should be triggered, False otherwise
        """
        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))
        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))
        side = position.get("side", "").lower()
        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:
            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for take-profit check.")
            return False

        take_profit_triggered = False
        if side == "long":
            if (current_price / entry_price - 1) >= self.take_profit_pct:
                take_profit_triggered = True
        elif side == "short":
            if (entry_price / current_price - 1) >= self.take_profit_pct:
                take_profit_triggered = True

        if take_profit_triggered:
            self.logger.info(f"Take-profit triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")
        return take_profit_triggered

BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n   data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. If you no longer need to take action, use the command ‘end’ to stop.'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The first four core files (main.py, config.py, exchange.py, strategy.py) have been successfully implemented according to the system design. Load configuration\n    config_path = "config.json"\n    try:\n        config = BotConfig.load(config_path)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to load config: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Exchange\n    exchange = Exchange()\n    try:\n        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Strategy\n    strategy = TrendStrategy(config.strategy_params)\n\n    # Initialize Risk Manager\n    risk_manager = RiskManager(config.risk_params)\n\n    # Initialize Data Feed\n    data_feed = DataFeed()\n    data_feed.subscribe(config.assets)\n\n    # Initialize Monitor and Notifier\n    monitor = Monitor()\n    notifier = Notifier(config.notification_settings)\n\n    async def on_data_update(symbol: str):\n        try:\n            data = data_feed.get_latest(symbol)\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(data)\n            positions = exchange.get_positions()\n            position = next((p for p in positions if p[\'symbol\'] == symbol), None)\n\n            risk_ok = risk_manager.check_risk(position, signal)\n            if risk_ok:\n                # Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. content:\nimport json\nimport os\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, root_validator\nfrom cryptography.fernet import Fernet\n\nclass BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Parameters for trend-following strategy")\n    risk_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Risk management parameters")\n    assets: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="List of trading symbols")\n    notification_settings: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Notification preferences")\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n        with open(path, "rb") as f:\n            data = f.read()\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.decrypt(data)\n        else:\n            data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n            raise ValueError(f"Invalid config file: {e}")\n\n    def save(self, path: str, encryption_key: str = None):\n        config_dict = self.dict()\n        data = json.dumps(config_dict, indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Fernet.generate_key().decode("utf-8")\n\n    @root_validator(pre=True)\n    def check_required_fields(cls, values):\n        required_fields = ["api_key", "api_secret", "strategy_params", "risk_params", "assets", "notification_settings"]\n        for field in required_fields:\n            if field not in values or values[field] is None:\n                raise ValueError(f"Missing required config field: {field}")\n        return values\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. 20% default\n        self.stop_loss_pct = float(self.params.get("stop_loss_pct", 0.05))  # 5% default\n        self.take_profit_pct = float(self.params.get("take_profit_pct", 0.1))  # 10% default\n        self.max_position_size = float(self.params.get("max_position_size", 1.0))  # 1 contract default\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("RiskManager")\n\n    def check_risk(self, position: Optional[Dict[str, Any]], signal: str) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if a new trade is allowed based on risk parameters.\n        :param position: Current position dict for the symbol (can be None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position is None:\n            self.logger.info("No open position, trade allowed.")\n            return True\n\n        # Check position size\n        position_size = float(position.get("contracts", 0))\n        if position_size >= self.max_position_size:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Position size {position_size} exceeds max {self.max_position_size}.")\n            return False\n\n        # Check drawdown (if available)\n        unrealized_pnl = float(position.get("unrealizedPnl", 0))\n        entry_value = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0)) * position_size if position.get("entryPrice") else 0\n        if entry_value > 0:\n            drawdown = -unrealized_pnl / entry_value\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.logger.warning(f"Drawdown {drawdown:.2%} exceeds max {self.max_drawdown:.2%}.")\n                return False\n\n        self.logger.info("Risk check passed, trade allowed.")\n        return True\n\n    def apply_stop_loss(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if stop-loss should be triggered for the position.\n        :param position: Current position dict\n        :return: True if stop-loss should be triggered, False otherwise\n        """\n        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))\n        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))\n        side = position.get("side", "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n        stop_loss_triggered = False\n        if side == "long":\n            if (current_price / entry_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:\n                stop_loss_triggered = True\n        elif side == "short":\n            if (entry_price / current_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:\n                stop_loss_triggered = True\n\n        if stop_loss_triggered:\n            self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n dict)\\n064|        +check_risk(position: dict, signal: str) -> bool\\n065|        +apply_stop_loss(position: dict) -> bool\\n066|        +apply_take_profit(position: dict) -> bool\\n067|    }\\n068|    class DataFeed {\\n069|        +subscribe(symbols: list[str])\\n070|        +get_latest(symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame\\n071|        +on_update(callback)\\n072|    }\\n073|    class Monitor {\\n074| Load configuration\n    config_path = "config.json"\n    try:\n        config = BotConfig.load(config_path)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to load config: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Exchange\n    exchange = Exchange()\n    try:\n        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Strategy\n    strategy = TrendStrategy(config.strategy_params)\n\n    # Initialize Risk Manager\n    risk_manager = RiskManager(config.risk_params)\n\n    # Initialize Data Feed\n    data_feed = DataFeed()\n    data_feed.subscribe(config.assets)\n\n    # Initialize Monitor and Notifier\n    monitor = Monitor()\n    notifier = Notifier(config.notification_settings)\n\n    async def on_data_update(symbol: str):\n        try:\n            data = data_feed.get_latest(symbol)\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(data)\n            positions = exchange.get_positions()\n            position = next((p for p in positions if p[\'symbol\'] == symbol), None)\n\n            risk_ok = risk_manager.check_risk(position, signal)\n            if risk_ok:\n                # Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Parameters for trend-following strategy")\n    risk_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Risk management parameters")\n    assets: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="List of trading symbols")\n    notification_settings: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Notification preferences")\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n        with open(path, "rb") as f:\n            data = f.read()\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.decrypt(data)\n        else:\n            data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n            raise ValueError(f"Invalid config file: {e}")\n\n    def save(self, path: str, encryption_key: str = None):\n        config_dict = self.dict()\n        data = json.dumps(config_dict, indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Fernet.generate_key().decode("utf-8")\n\n    @root_validator(pre=True)\n    def check_required_fields(cls, values):\n        required_fields = ["api_key", "api_secret", "strategy_params", "risk_params", "assets", "notification_settings"]\n        for field in required_fields:\n            if field not in values or values[field] is None:\n                raise ValueError(f"Missing required config field: {field}")\n        return values\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished.
