Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/data.py has been successfully created, with content:
import asyncio
import logging
from typing import List, Callable, Dict, Any, Optional
import threading
import pandas as pd
import ccxt
import time

class DataFeed:
    """
    DataFeed class for subscribing to symbols, retrieving latest market data,
    and handling update callbacks. Uses polling via ccxt for CoinDCX Futures.
    """

    def __init__(self, poll_interval: float = 2.0):
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DataFeed")
        self.symbols: List[str] = []
        self.callbacks: List[Callable[[str], None]] = []
        self.latest_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame] = {}
        self.poll_interval = poll_interval
        self._running = False
        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None
        self._exchange: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None

    def subscribe(self, symbols: List[str]):
        """
        Subscribe to a list of trading symbols.
        """
        self.symbols = list(set(self.symbols) | set(symbols))
        self.logger.info(f"Subscribed to symbols: {self.symbols}")

    def set_exchange(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx):
        """
        Set the ccxt exchange client for data fetching.
        """
        self._exchange = exchange

    def get_latest(self, symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
        """
        Get the latest OHLCV data for a symbol.
        """
        if symbol in self.latest_data:
            return self.latest_data[symbol]
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"No data available for symbol: {symbol}")

    def on_update(self, callback: Callable[[str], None]):
        """
        Register a callback to be called when new data is available for a symbol.
        The callback receives the symbol as argument.
        """
        self.callbacks.append(callback)

    def _fetch_ohlcv(self, symbol: str, timeframe: str = "1m", limit: int = 100) -> pd.DataFrame:
        """
        Fetch OHLCV data for a symbol using ccxt.
        """
        if not self._exchange:
            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client not set in DataFeed.")
        try:
            ohlcv = self._exchange.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)
            df = pd.DataFrame(ohlcv, columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])
            df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"], unit="ms")
            return df
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching OHLCV for {symbol}: {e}")
            return pd.DataFrame(columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])

    def _polling_loop(self):
        """
        Polling loop to fetch data and trigger callbacks.
        """
        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop started.")
        while self._running:
            for symbol in self.symbols:
                df = self._fetch_ohlcv(symbol)
                if not df.empty:
                    prev_df = self.latest_data.get(symbol)
                    # Only trigger update if new data is available
                    if prev_df is None or not df.equals(prev_df):
                        self.latest_data[symbol] = df
                        for cb in self.callbacks:
                            try:
                                cb(symbol)
                            except Exception as e:
                                self.logger.error(f"Error in data update callback for {symbol}: {e}")
            time.sleep(self.poll_interval)
        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")

    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):
        """
        Start the data feed polling loop in a background thread.
        Optionally set the exchange client.
        """
        if exchange:
            self.set_exchange(exchange)
        if not self._exchange:
            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client must be set before starting DataFeed.")
        if self._running:
            self.logger.warning("DataFeed already running.")
            return
        self._running = True
        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)
        self._thread.start()
        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")

    def stop(self):
        """
        Stop the data feed polling loop.
        """
        self._running = False
        if self._thread:
            self._thread.join()
            self._thread = None
        self.logger.info("DataFeed stopped.")

Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. If you no longer need to take action, use the command ‘end’ to stop.'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The first four core files (main.py, config.py, exchange.py, strategy.py) have been successfully implemented according to the system design. }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/risk.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional\n\nclass RiskManager:\n    """\n    Implements risk management for the trading bot.\n    Includes check_risk, apply_stop_loss, and apply_take_profit methods.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params = params.copy()\n        self.max_drawdown = float(self.params.get("max_drawdown", 0.2))  # 20% default\n        self.stop_loss_pct = float(self.params.get("stop_loss_pct", 0.05))  # 5% default\n        self.take_profit_pct = float(self.params.get("take_profit_pct", 0.1))  # 10% default\n        self.max_position_size = float(self.params.get("max_position_size", 1.0))  # 1 contract default\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("RiskManager")\n\n    def check_risk(self, position: Optional[Dict[str, Any]], signal: str) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if a new trade is allowed based on risk parameters.\n        :param position: Current position dict for the symbol (can be None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position is None:\n            self.logger.info("No open position, trade allowed.")\n            return True\n\n        # Check position size\n        position_size = float(position.get("contracts", 0))\n        if position_size >= self.max_position_size:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Position size {position_size} exceeds max {self.max_position_size}.")\n            return False\n\n        # Check drawdown (if available)\n        unrealized_pnl = float(position.get("unrealizedPnl", 0))\n        entry_value = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0)) * position_size if position.get("entryPrice") else 0\n        if entry_value > 0:\n            drawdown = -unrealized_pnl / entry_value\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.logger.warning(f"Drawdown {drawdown:.2%} exceeds max {self.max_drawdown:.2%}.")\n                return False\n\n        self.logger.info("Risk check passed, trade allowed.")\n        return True\n\n    def apply_stop_loss(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if stop-loss should be triggered for the position.\n        :param position: Current position dict\n        :return: True if stop-loss should be triggered, False otherwise\n        """\n        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))\n        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))\n        side = position.get("side", "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n        stop_loss_triggered = False\n        if side == "long":\n            if (current_price / entry_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:\n                stop_loss_triggered = True\n        elif side == "short":\n            if (entry_price / current_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:\n                stop_loss_triggered = True\n\n        if stop_loss_triggered:\n            self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n     """\n        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))\n        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))\n        side = position.get("side", "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for take-profit check.")\n            return False\n\n Uses polling via ccxt for CoinDCX Futures.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, poll_interval: float = 2.0):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DataFeed")\n        self.symbols: List[str] = []\n        self.callbacks: List[Callable[[str], None]] = []\n        self.latest_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame] = {}\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self._running = False\n        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None\n        self._exchange: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None\n\n    def subscribe(self, symbols: List[str]):\n        """\n        Subscribe to a list of trading symbols.\n        """\n        self.symbols = list(set(self.symbols) | set(symbols))\n        self.logger.info(f"Subscribed to symbols: {self.symbols}")\n\n    def set_exchange(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx):\n        """\n        Set the ccxt exchange client for data fetching.\n        """\n        self._exchange = exchange\n\n    def get_latest(self, symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Get the latest OHLCV data for a symbol.\n        """\n        if symbol in self.latest_data:\n            return self.latest_data[symbol]\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f"No data available for symbol: {symbol}")\n\n    def on_update(self, callback: Callable[[str], None]):\n        """\n        Register a callback to be called when new data is available for a symbol.\n        The callback receives the symbol as argument.\n        """\n        self.callbacks.append(callback)\n\n    def _fetch_ohlcv(self, symbol: str, timeframe: str = "1m", limit: int = 100) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Fetch OHLCV data for a symbol using ccxt.\n        """\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client not set in DataFeed.")\n        try:\n            ohlcv = self._exchange.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)\n            df = pd.DataFrame(ohlcv, columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n            df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"], unit="ms")\n            return df\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching OHLCV for {symbol}: {e}")\n            return pd.DataFrame(columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n\n    def _polling_loop(self):\n        """\n        Polling loop to fetch data and trigger callbacks.\n        """\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop started.")\n        while self._running:\n            for symbol in self.symbols:\n                df = self._fetch_ohlcv(symbol)\n                if not df.empty:\n                    prev_df = self.latest_data.get(symbol)\n                    # Only trigger update if new data is available\n                    if prev_df is None or not df.equals(prev_df):\n                        self.latest_data[symbol] = df\n                        for cb in self.callbacks:\n                            try:\n                                cb(symbol)\n                            except Exception as e:\n                                self.logger.error(f"Error in data update callback for {symbol}: {e}")\n            time.sleep(self.poll_interval)\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")\n\n    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):\n        """\n        Start the data feed polling loop in a background thread.\n        Optionally set the exchange client.\n        """\n        if exchange:\n            self.set_exchange(exchange)\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client must be set before starting DataFeed.")\n        if self._running:\n            self.logger.warning("DataFeed already running.")\n            return\n        self._running = True\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)\n        self._thread.start()\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the data feed polling loop.\n        """\n        self._running = False\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join()\n            self._thread = None\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed stopped.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n        with open(path, "rb") as f:\n            data = f.read()\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.decrypt(data)\n        else:\n            data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. import logging
from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional
import threading
import time

class Monitor:
    """
    Monitor class for logging trades, sending alerts, and reporting status.
    Includes log_trade, alert, and get_status methods as per system design.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("Monitor")
        self._trade_log: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        self._alerts: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        self._status: Dict[str, Any] = {
            "running": True,
            "last_trade": None,
            "last_alert": None,
            "start_time": time.time(),
            "trade_count": 0,
            "alert_count": 0,
        }
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def log_trade(self, trade: Dict[str, Any]):
        """
        Log a trade event.
        :param trade: Trade information dictionary.
        """
        with self._lock:
            self._trade_log.append(trade)
            self._status["last_trade"] = trade
            self._status["trade_count"] += 1
        self.logger.info(f"Trade logged: {trade}")

    def alert(self, event: str):
        """
        Log an alert event.
        :param event: Alert message string.
        """
        alert_info = {
            "timestamp": time.time(),
            "event": event
        }
        with self._lock:
            self._alerts.append(alert_info)
            self._status["last_alert"] = alert_info
            self._status["alert_count"] += 1
        self.logger.warning(f"ALERT: {event}")

    def get_status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get current bot status.
        :return: Status dictionary.
        """
        with self._lock:
            status_copy = self._status.copy()
            status_copy["uptime_sec"] = time.time() - self._status["start_time"]
        return status_copy

    def get_logs(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Get all trade logs.
        :return: List of trade log dictionaries.
        """
        with self._lock:
            return list(self._trade_log)

    def get_alerts(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Get all alert logs.
        :return: List of alert log dictionaries.
        """
        with self._lock:
            return list(self._alerts)

an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Otherwise, finish current task by Plan.finish_current_task explicitly.\n3. Each time you finish a task, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to report your progress.\n4. Don\'t forget to append task first when all existing tasks are finished and new tasks are required.\n5. Avoid repeating tasks you have already completed. And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. If you no longer need to take action, use the command ‘end’ to stop.'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The first four core files (main.py, config.py, exchange.py, strategy.py) have been successfully implemented according to the system design. }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/risk.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional\n\nclass RiskManager:\n    """\n    Implements risk management for the trading bot.\n    Includes check_risk, apply_stop_loss, and apply_take_profit methods.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params = params.copy()\n        self.max_drawdown = float(self.params.get("max_drawdown", 0.2))  # 20% default\n        self.stop_loss_pct = float(self.params.get("stop_loss_pct", 0.05))  # 5% default\n        self.take_profit_pct = float(self.params.get("take_profit_pct", 0.1))  # 10% default\n        self.max_position_size = float(self.params.get("max_position_size", 1.0))  # 1 contract default\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("RiskManager")\n\n    def check_risk(self, position: Optional[Dict[str, Any]], signal: str) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if a new trade is allowed based on risk parameters.\n        :param position: Current position dict for the symbol (can be None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position is None:\n            self.logger.info("No open position, trade allowed.")\n            return True\n\n        # Check position size\n        position_size = float(position.get("contracts", 0))\n        if position_size >= self.max_position_size:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Position size {position_size} exceeds max {self.max_position_size}.")\n            return False\n\n        # Check drawdown (if available)\n        unrealized_pnl = float(position.get("unrealizedPnl", 0))\n        entry_value = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0)) * position_size if position.get("entryPrice") else 0\n        if entry_value > 0:\n            drawdown = -unrealized_pnl / entry_value\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.logger.warning(f"Drawdown {drawdown:.2%} exceeds max {self.max_drawdown:.2%}.")\n                return False\n\n        self.logger.info("Risk check passed, trade allowed.")\n        return True\n\n    def apply_stop_loss(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if stop-loss should be triggered for the position.\n        :param position: Current position dict\n        :return: True if stop-loss should be triggered, False otherwise\n        """\n        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))\n        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))\n        side = position.get("side", "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n        stop_loss_triggered = False\n        if side == "long":\n            if (current_price / entry_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:\n                stop_loss_triggered = True\n        elif side == "short":\n            if (entry_price / current_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:\n                stop_loss_triggered = True\n\n        if stop_loss_triggered:\n            self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if take-profit should be triggered for the position.\n        :param position: Current position dict\n        :return: True if take-profit should be triggered, False otherwise\n        """\n        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))\n        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))\n        side = position.get("side", "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for take-profit check.")\n            return False\n\n        take_profit_triggered = False\n        if side == "long":\n            if (current_price / entry_price - 1) >= self.take_profit_pct:\n                take_profit_triggered = True\n        elif side == "short":\n            if (entry_price / current_price - 1) >= self.take_profit_pct:\n                take_profit_triggered = True\n\n        if take_profit_triggered:\n Uses polling via ccxt for CoinDCX Futures.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, poll_interval: float = 2.0):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DataFeed")\n        self.symbols: List[str] = []\n        self.callbacks: List[Callable[[str], None]] = []\n        self.latest_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame] = {}\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self._running = False\n        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None\n        self._exchange: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None\n\n    def subscribe(self, symbols: List[str]):\n        """\n        Subscribe to a list of trading symbols.\n        """\n        self.symbols = list(set(self.symbols) | set(symbols))\n        self.logger.info(f"Subscribed to symbols: {self.symbols}")\n\n    def set_exchange(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx):\n        """\n        Set the ccxt exchange client for data fetching.\n        """\n        self._exchange = exchange\n\n    def get_latest(self, symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Get the latest OHLCV data for a symbol.\n        """\n        if symbol in self.latest_data:\n            return self.latest_data[symbol]\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f"No data available for symbol: {symbol}")\n\n    def on_update(self, callback: Callable[[str], None]):\n        """\n        Register a callback to be called when new data is available for a symbol.\n        The callback receives the symbol as argument.\n        """\n        self.callbacks.append(callback)\n\n    def _fetch_ohlcv(self, symbol: str, timeframe: str = "1m", limit: int = 100) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Fetch OHLCV data for a symbol using ccxt.\n        """\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client not set in DataFeed.")\n        try:\n            ohlcv = self._exchange.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)\n            df = pd.DataFrame(ohlcv, columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n            df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"], unit="ms")\n            return df\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching OHLCV for {symbol}: {e}")\n            return pd.DataFrame(columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n\n    def _polling_loop(self):\n        """\n        Polling loop to fetch data and trigger callbacks.\n        """\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop started.")\n        while self._running:\n            for symbol in self.symbols:\n                df = self._fetch_ohlcv(symbol)\n                if not df.empty:\n                    prev_df = self.latest_data.get(symbol)\n                    # Only trigger update if new data is available\n                    if prev_df is None or not df.equals(prev_df):\n                        self.latest_data[symbol] = df\n                        for cb in self.callbacks:\n                            try:\n                                cb(symbol)\n                            except Exception as e:\n                                self.logger.error(f"Error in data update callback for {symbol}: {e}")\n            time.sleep(self.poll_interval)\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")\n\n    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):\n        """\n        Start the data feed polling loop in a background thread.\n        Optionally set the exchange client.\n        """\n        if exchange:\n            self.set_exchange(exchange)\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client must be set before starting DataFeed.")\n        if self._running:\n            self.logger.warning("DataFeed already running.")\n            return\n        self._running = True\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)\n        self._thread.start()\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the data feed polling loop.\n        """\n        self._running = False\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join()\n            self._thread = None\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed stopped.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n     time.sleep(self.poll_interval)\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")\n\n    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):\n        """\n        Start the data feed polling loop in a background thread.\n        Optionally set the exchange client.\n        """\n        if exchange:\n            self.set_exchange(exchange)\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise self._running = True\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)\n        self._thread.start()\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the data feed polling loop.\n        """\n        self._running = False\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join()\n            self._thread = List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        self._alerts: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        self._status: Dict[str, Any] = {\n            "running": True,\n            "last_trade": None,\n            "last_alert": None,\n            "start_time": time.time(),\n            "trade_count": 0,\n            "alert_count": 0,\n        }\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    def log_trade(self, Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/notifier.py has been successfully created, with content:
import logging
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional
import requests

class Notifier:
    """
    Notifier class for sending notifications via email, SMS, and push.
    Includes send_email, send_sms, and send_push methods as per system design.
    """

    def __init__(self, notification_settings: Dict[str, Any]):
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("Notifier")
        self.email_settings = notification_settings.get("email", {})
        self.sms_settings = notification_settings.get("sms", {})
        self.push_settings = notification_settings.get("push", {})

    def send_email(self, msg: str, subject: str = "Trading Bot Notification", to: Optional[str] = None):
        """
        Send an email notification.
        :param msg: Message body
        :param subject: Email subject
        :param to: Recipient email address (optional, uses default if not provided)
        """
        smtp_server = self.email_settings.get("smtp_server")
        smtp_port = self.email_settings.get("smtp_port", 587)
        smtp_user = self.email_settings.get("smtp_user")
        smtp_password = self.email_settings.get("smtp_password")
        from_addr = self.email_settings.get("from_addr")
        to_addr = to or self.email_settings.get("to_addr")

        if not all([smtp_server, smtp_user, smtp_password, from_addr, to_addr]):
            self.logger.error("Email settings are incomplete.")
            return

        try:
            message = MIMEText(msg)
            message["Subject"] = subject
            message["From"] = from_addr
            message["To"] = to_addr

            with smtplib.SMTP(smtp_server, smtp_port) as server:
                server.starttls()
                server.login(smtp_user, smtp_password)
                server.sendmail(from_addr, [to_addr], message.as_string())
            self.logger.info(f"Email sent to {to_addr}: {subject}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to send email: {e}")

    def send_sms(self, msg: str, to: Optional[str] = None):
        """
        Send an SMS notification.
        :param msg: Message body
        :param to: Recipient phone number (optional, uses default if not provided)
        """
        sms_api_url = self.sms_settings.get("api_url")
        sms_api_key = self.sms_settings.get("api_key")
        sender_id = self.sms_settings.get("sender_id")
        to_number = to or self.sms_settings.get("to_number")

        if not all([sms_api_url, sms_api_key, sender_id, to_number]):
            self.logger.error("SMS settings are incomplete.")
            return

        payload = {
            "api_key": sms_api_key,
            "sender": sender_id,
            "to": to_number,
            "message": msg
        }

        try:
            response = requests.post(sms_api_url, json=payload, timeout=10)
            if response.status_code == 200:
                self.logger.info(f"SMS sent to {to_number}: {msg}")
            else:
                self.logger.error(f"Failed to send SMS: {response.text}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to send SMS: {e}")

    def send_push(self, msg: str, title: str = "Trading Bot Alert"):
        """
        Send a push notification.
        :param msg: Message body
        :param title: Notification title
        """
        push_api_url = self.push_settings.get("api_url")
        push_api_key = self.push_settings.get("api_key")
        device_id = self.push_settings.get("device_id")

        if not all([push_api_url, push_api_key, device_id]):
            self.logger.error("Push notification settings are incomplete.")
            return

        payload = {
            "api_key": push_api_key,
            "device_id": device_id,
            "title": title,
            "message": msg
        }

        try:
            response = requests.post(push_api_url, json=payload, timeout=10)
            if response.status_code == 200:
                self.logger.info(f"Push notification sent: {title} - {msg}")
            else:
                self.logger.error(f"Failed to send push notification: {response.text}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to send push notification: {e}")

Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. If you no longer need to take action, use the command ‘end’ to stop.'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The first four core files (main.py, config.py, exchange.py, strategy.py) have been successfully implemented according to the system design. Includes check_risk, apply_stop_loss, and apply_take_profit methods as per system design."\n        }\n    },\n    {\n        "command_name": "Plan.finish_current_task",\n        "args": {}\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/risk.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional\n\nclass RiskManager:\n    """\n    Implements risk management for the trading bot.\n    Includes check_risk, apply_stop_loss, and apply_take_profit methods.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params = params.copy()\n        self.max_drawdown = float(self.params.get("max_drawdown", 0.2))  # 20% default\n        self.stop_loss_pct = float(self.params.get("stop_loss_pct", 0.05))  # 5% default\n        self.take_profit_pct = float(self.params.get("take_profit_pct", 0.1))  # 10% default\n        self.max_position_size = float(self.params.get("max_position_size", 1.0))  # 1 contract default\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("RiskManager")\n\n    def check_risk(self, position: Optional[Dict[str, Any]], signal: str) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if a new trade is allowed based on risk parameters.\n        :param position: Current position dict for the symbol (can be None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position is None:\n            self.logger.info("No open position, trade allowed.")\n            return True\n\n        # Check position size\n        position_size = float(position.get("contracts", 0))\n        if position_size >= self.max_position_size:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Position size {position_size} exceeds max {self.max_position_size}.")\n            return False\n\n        # Check drawdown (if available)\n        unrealized_pnl = float(position.get("unrealizedPnl", 0))\n        entry_value = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0)) * position_size if position.get("entryPrice") else 0\n        if entry_value > 0:\n            drawdown = -unrealized_pnl / entry_value\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.logger.warning(f"Drawdown {drawdown:.2%} exceeds max {self.max_drawdown:.2%}.")\n                return False\n\n        self.logger.info("Risk check passed, trade allowed.")\n        return True\n\n    def apply_stop_loss(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if stop-loss should be triggered for the position.\n        :param position: Current position dict\n        :return: True if stop-loss should be triggered, False otherwise\n        """\n        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))\n        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))\n        side = position.get("side", "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n        stop_loss_triggered = False\n        if side == "long":\n            if (current_price / entry_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:\n                stop_loss_triggered = True\n        elif side == "short":\n            if (entry_price / current_price - 1) <= -self.stop_loss_pct:\n                stop_loss_triggered = True\n\n        if stop_loss_triggered:\n            self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if take-profit should be triggered for the position.\n        :param position: Current position dict\n        :return: True if take-profit should be triggered, False otherwise\n        """\n        entry_price = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0))\n        current_price = float(position.get("markPrice", 0))\n        side = position.get("side", "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for take-profit check.")\n            return False\n\n        take_profit_triggered = False\n        if side == "long":\n            if (current_price / entry_price - 1) >= self.take_profit_pct:\n                take_profit_triggered = True\n        elif side == "short":\n            if (entry_price / current_price - 1) >= self.take_profit_pct:\n                take_profit_triggered = True\n\n        if take_profit_triggered:\n            self.logger.info(f"Take-profit triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return take_profit_triggered\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. """\n        Get the latest OHLCV data for a symbol.\n        """\n        if symbol in self.latest_data:\n            return self.latest_data[symbol]\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f"No data available for symbol: {symbol}")\n\n    def on_update(self, callback: Callable[[str], None]):\n        """\n        Register a callback to be called when new data is available for a symbol.\n        The callback receives the symbol as argument.\n        """\n        self.callbacks.append(callback)\n\n    def _fetch_ohlcv(self, symbol: str, timeframe: str = "1m", limit: int = 100) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Fetch OHLCV data for a symbol using ccxt.\n        """\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client not set in DataFeed.")\n        try:\n            ohlcv = self._exchange.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)\n            df = pd.DataFrame(ohlcv, columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", {symbol}: {e}")\n            return pd.DataFrame(columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n\n    def _polling_loop(self):\n        """\n        Polling loop to fetch data and     time.sleep(self.poll_interval)\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")\n\n    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):\n        """\n        Start the data feed polling self._running = True\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)\n        self._thread.start()\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")\n\n    def Load configuration\n    config_path = "config.json"\n    try:\n        config = BotConfig.load(config_path)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to load config: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Exchange\n    exchange = Exchange()\n    try:\n        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Strategy\n    strategy = TrendStrategy(config.strategy_params)\n\n    # Initialize Risk Manager\n    risk_manager = RiskManager(config.risk_params)\n\n    # Initialize Data Feed\n    data_feed = DataFeed()\n    data_feed.subscribe(config.assets)\n\n    # Initialize Monitor and Notifier\n    monitor = Monitor()\n    notifier = Notifier(config.notification_settings)\n\n    async def on_data_update(symbol: str):\n        try:\n            data = data_feed.get_latest(symbol)\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(data)\n            positions = exchange.get_positions()\n            position = next((p for p in positions if p[\'symbol\'] == symbol), None)\n\n            risk_ok = risk_manager.check_risk(position, signal)\n            if risk_ok:\n                # Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. content:\nimport json\nimport os\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, root_validator\nfrom cryptography.fernet import Fernet\n\nclass BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Parameters for trend-following strategy")\n    risk_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Risk management parameters")\n    assets: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="List of trading symbols")\n    notification_settings: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Notification preferences")\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n        with open(path, "rb") as f:\n            data = f.read()\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.decrypt(data)\n        else:\n            data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n            raise ValueError(f"Invalid config file: {e}")\n\n    def save(self, path: str, encryption_key: str = None):\n        config_dict = self.dict()\n        data = json.dumps(config_dict, indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Fernet.generate_key().decode("utf-8")\n\n    @root_validator(pre=True)\n    def check_required_fields(cls, values):\n        required_fields = ["api_key", "api_secret", "strategy_params", "risk_params", "assets", "notification_settings"]\n        for field in required_fields:\n            if field not in values or values[field] is None:\n                raise ValueError(f"Missing required config field: {field}")\n        return values\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Optional[Dict[str, Any]], signal: str) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if a new trade is allowed based on risk parameters.\n        :param position: Current position dict for the symbol (can be None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position is None:\n            self.logger.info("No open position, trade allowed.")\n            return True\n\n        # Check position size\n        position_size = float(position.get("contracts", 0))\n        if position_size >= self.max_position_size:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Position size {position_size} exceeds max {self.max_position_size}.")\n            return False\n\n        # Check drawdown (if available)\n        unrealized_pnl = float(position.get("unrealizedPnl", 0))\n        entry_value = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0)) * position_size if position.get("entryPrice") else 0\n        if entry_value > 0:\n            drawdown = -unrealized_pnl / entry_value\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.logger.warning(f"Drawdown {drawdown:.2%} exceeds max {self.max_drawdown:.2%}.")\n                return False\n\n        self.logger.info("Risk check passed, trade allowed.")\n        return True\n\n    def apply_stop_loss(self, position: "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n Uses polling via ccxt for CoinDCX Futures.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, poll_interval: float = 2.0):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DataFeed")\n        self.symbols: List[str] = []\n        self.callbacks: List[Callable[[str], None]] = []\n        self.latest_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame] = {}\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self._running = False\n        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None\n        self._exchange: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None\n\n    def subscribe(self, symbols: List[str]):\n        """\n        Subscribe to a list of trading symbols.\n        """\n        self.symbols = list(set(self.symbols) | set(symbols))\n        self.logger.info(f"Subscribed to symbols: {self.symbols}")\n\n    def set_exchange(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx):\n        """\n        Set the ccxt exchange client for data fetching.\n        """\n        self._exchange = exchange\n\n    def get_latest(self, symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Get the latest OHLCV data for a symbol.\n        """\n        if symbol in self.latest_data:\n            return self.latest_data[symbol]\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f"No data available for symbol: {symbol}")\n\n    def on_update(self, callback: Callable[[str], None]):\n        """\n        Register a callback to be called when new data is available for a symbol.\n        The callback receives the symbol as argument.\n        """\n        self.callbacks.append(callback)\n\n    def _fetch_ohlcv(self, symbol: str, timeframe: str = "1m", limit: int = 100) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Fetch OHLCV data for a symbol using ccxt.\n        """\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client not set in DataFeed.")\n        try:\n            ohlcv = self._exchange.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)\n            df = pd.DataFrame(ohlcv, columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n            df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"], unit="ms")\n            return df\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching OHLCV for {symbol}: {e}")\n            return pd.DataFrame(columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n\n    def _polling_loop(self):\n        """\n        Polling loop to fetch data and trigger callbacks.\n        """\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop started.")\n        while self._running:\n            for symbol in self.symbols:\n                df = self._fetch_ohlcv(symbol)\n                if not df.empty:\n                    prev_df = self.latest_data.get(symbol)\n                    # Only trigger update if new data is available\n                    if prev_df is None or not df.equals(prev_df):\n                        self.latest_data[symbol] = df\n                        for cb in self.callbacks:\n                            try:\n                                cb(symbol)\n                            except Exception as e:\n                                self.logger.error(f"Error in data update callback for {symbol}: {e}")\n            time.sleep(self.poll_interval)\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")\n\n    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):\n        """\n        Start the data feed polling loop in a background thread.\n        Optionally set the exchange client.\n        """\n        if exchange:\n            self.set_exchange(exchange)\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client must be set before starting DataFeed.")\n        if self._running:\n            self.logger.warning("DataFeed already running.")\n            return\n        self._running = True\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)\n        self._thread.start()\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the data feed polling loop.\n        """\n        self._running = False\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join()\n            self._thread = None\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed stopped.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished.
