content:\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional\nimport threading\nimport time\n\nclass Monitor:\n    """\n    Monitor class for logging trades, sending alerts, and reporting status.\n    Includes log_trade, alert, and get_status methods as per system design.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("Monitor")\n        self._trade_log: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        self._alerts: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        self._status: Dict[str, Any] = {\n            "running": True,\n            "last_trade": None,\n            "last_alert": None,\n            "start_time": time.time(),\n            "trade_count": 0,\n            "alert_count": 0,\n        }\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    def log_trade(self, trade: Dict[str, Any]):\n        """\n        Log a trade event.\n        :param trade: Trade information dictionary.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            self._trade_log.append(trade)\n            self._status["last_trade"] = trade\n            self._status["trade_count"] += 1\n        self.logger.info(f"Trade logged: {trade}")\n\n    def alert(self, event: str):\n        """\n        Log an alert event.\n        :param event: Alert message string.\n        """\n        alert_info = {\n            "timestamp": time.time(),\n            "event": event\n        }\n        with self._lock:\n            self._alerts.append(alert_info)\n            self._status["last_alert"] = alert_info\n            self._status["alert_count"] += 1\n        self.logger.warning(f"ALERT: {event}")\n\n    def get_status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Get current bot status.\n        :return: Status dictionary.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            status_copy = self._status.copy()\n            status_copy["uptime_sec"] = time.time() - self._status["start_time"]\n        return status_copy\n\n    def get_logs(self) -> Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/backtest.py has been successfully created, with content:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional
from strategy import TrendStrategy
from risk import RiskManager

class Backtester:
    """
    Backtester class for running strategy backtests and reporting results.
    Includes run and report methods as per system design.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.results: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        self.trades: list = []
        self.equity_curve: list = []
        self.initial_balance: float = 10000.0  # Default initial balance for backtest
        self.final_balance: float = self.initial_balance
        self.max_drawdown: float = 0.0
        self.total_return: float = 0.0
        self.win_rate: float = 0.0
        self.total_trades: int = 0
        self.profitable_trades: int = 0

    def run(self, strategy: TrendStrategy, data: pd.DataFrame, risk: RiskManager) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Run backtest for the given strategy and data, using the provided risk manager.
        :param strategy: TrendStrategy instance
        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data
        :param risk: RiskManager instance
        :return: Results dictionary
        """
        balance = self.initial_balance
        position = None
        entry_price = 0.0
        position_side = None
        position_size = 0.0
        self.trades = []
        self.equity_curve = [balance]
        self.max_drawdown = 0.0
        self.total_trades = 0
        self.profitable_trades = 0

        for i in range(1, len(data)):
            window = data.iloc[:i+1]
            signal = strategy.generate_signal(window)
            price = window["close"].iloc[-1]
            timestamp = window["timestamp"].iloc[-1] if "timestamp" in window.columns else i

            # Simulate position management
            if position is None and signal in ["long", "short"]:
                # Open new position
                position = {
                    "side": signal,
                    "entryPrice": price,
                    "markPrice": price,
                    "contracts": 1,
                    "timestamp": timestamp
                }
                entry_price = price
                position_side = signal
                position_size = 1
                self.total_trades += 1
            elif position is not None:
                # Update mark price
                position["markPrice"] = price

                # Check for stop-loss/take-profit
                stop_loss_triggered = risk.apply_stop_loss(position)
                take_profit_triggered = risk.apply_take_profit(position)

                # Close position if triggered or opposite signal
                close_position = False
                reason = ""
                if stop_loss_triggered:
                    close_position = True
                    reason = "stop_loss"
                elif take_profit_triggered:
                    close_position = True
                    reason = "take_profit"
                elif signal != position_side and signal in ["long", "short"]:
                    close_position = True
                    reason = "signal_flip"

                if close_position:
                    # Calculate PnL
                    pnl = 0.0
                    if position_side == "long":
                        pnl = (price - entry_price) * position_size
                    elif position_side == "short":
                        pnl = (entry_price - price) * position_size
                    balance += pnl
                    self.trades.append({
                        "entry_price": entry_price,
                        "exit_price": price,
                        "side": position_side,
                        "pnl": pnl,
                        "reason": reason,
                        "entry_time": position["timestamp"],
                        "exit_time": timestamp
                    })
                    if pnl > 0:
                        self.profitable_trades += 1
                    position = None
                    entry_price = 0.0
                    position_side = None
                    position_size = 0.0

            self.equity_curve.append(balance)
            # Update max drawdown
            peak = max(self.equity_curve)
            drawdown = (peak - balance) / peak if peak > 0 else 0
            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:
                self.max_drawdown = drawdown

        self.final_balance = balance
        self.total_return = (self.final_balance - self.initial_balance) / self.initial_balance
        self.win_rate = (self.profitable_trades / self.total_trades) if self.total_trades > 0 else 0.0

        self.results = {
            "initial_balance": self.initial_balance,
            "final_balance": self.final_balance,
            "total_return": self.total_return,
            "max_drawdown": self.max_drawdown,
            "total_trades": self.total_trades,
            "win_rate": self.win_rate,
            "trades": self.trades,
            "equity_curve": self.equity_curve
        }
        return self.results

    def report(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Return the backtest results.
        :return: Results dictionary
        """
        return self.results

Load configuration\n    config_path = "config.json"\n    try:\n        config = BotConfig.load(config_path)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to load config: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Exchange\n    exchange = Exchange()\n    try:\n        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize Strategy\n    strategy = TrendStrategy(config.strategy_params)\n\n    # Initialize Risk Manager\n    risk_manager = RiskManager(config.risk_params)\n\n    # Initialize Data Feed\n    data_feed = DataFeed()\n    data_feed.subscribe(config.assets)\n\n    # Initialize Monitor and Notifier\n    monitor = Monitor()\n    notifier = Notifier(config.notification_settings)\n\n    async def on_data_update(symbol: str):\n        try:\n            data = data_feed.get_latest(symbol)\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(data)\n            positions = exchange.get_positions()\n            position = next((p for p in positions if p[\'symbol\'] == symbol), None)\n\n            risk_ok = risk_manager.check_risk(position, signal)\n            if risk_ok:\n                # Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. content:\nimport json\nimport os\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, root_validator\nfrom cryptography.fernet import Fernet\n\nclass BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Parameters for trend-following strategy")\n    risk_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Risk management parameters")\n    assets: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="List of trading symbols")\n    notification_settings: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Notification preferences")\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n        with open(path, "rb") as f:\n            data = f.read()\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.decrypt(data)\n        else:\n            data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n            raise ValueError(f"Invalid config file: {e}")\n\n    def save(self, path: str, encryption_key: str = None):\n        config_dict = self.dict()\n        data = json.dumps(config_dict, indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Fernet.generate_key().decode("utf-8")\n\n    @root_validator(pre=True)\n    def check_required_fields(cls, values):\n        required_fields = ["api_key", "api_secret", "strategy_params", "risk_params", "assets", "notification_settings"]\n        for field in required_fields:\n            if field not in values or values[field] is None:\n                raise ValueError(f"Missing required config field: {field}")\n        return values\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Optional[Dict[str, Any]], signal: str) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if a new trade is allowed based on risk parameters.\n        :param position: Current position dict for the symbol (can be None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position is None:\n            self.logger.info("No open position, trade allowed.")\n            return True\n\n        # Check position size\n        position_size = float(position.get("contracts", 0))\n        if position_size >= self.max_position_size:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Position size {position_size} exceeds max {self.max_position_size}.")\n            return False\n\n        # Check drawdown (if available)\n        unrealized_pnl = float(position.get("unrealizedPnl", 0))\n        entry_value = float(position.get("entryPrice", 0)) * position_size if position.get("entryPrice") else 0\n        if entry_value > 0:\n            drawdown = -unrealized_pnl / entry_value\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.logger.warning(f"Drawdown {drawdown:.2%} exceeds max {self.max_drawdown:.2%}.")\n                return False\n\n        self.logger.info("Risk check passed, trade allowed.")\n        return True\n\n    def apply_stop_loss(self, position: "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n Uses polling via ccxt for CoinDCX Futures.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, poll_interval: float = 2.0):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DataFeed")\n        self.symbols: List[str] = []\n        self.callbacks: List[Callable[[str], None]] = []\n        self.latest_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame] = {}\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self._running = False\n        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None\n        self._exchange: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None\n\n    def subscribe(self, symbols: List[str]):\n        """\n        Subscribe to a list of trading symbols.\n        """\n        self.symbols = list(set(self.symbols) | set(symbols))\n        self.logger.info(f"Subscribed to symbols: {self.symbols}")\n\n    def set_exchange(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx):\n        """\n        Set the ccxt exchange client for data fetching.\n        """\n        self._exchange = exchange\n\n    def get_latest(self, symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Get the latest OHLCV data for a symbol.\n        """\n        if symbol in self.latest_data:\n            return self.latest_data[symbol]\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f"No data available for symbol: {symbol}")\n\n    def on_update(self, callback: Callable[[str], None]):\n        """\n        Register a callback to be called when new data is available for a symbol.\n        The callback receives the symbol as argument.\n        """\n        self.callbacks.append(callback)\n\n    def _fetch_ohlcv(self, symbol: str, timeframe: str = "1m", limit: int = 100) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Fetch OHLCV data for a symbol using ccxt.\n        """\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client not set in DataFeed.")\n        try:\n            ohlcv = self._exchange.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)\n            df = pd.DataFrame(ohlcv, columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n            df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"], unit="ms")\n            return df\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching OHLCV for {symbol}: {e}")\n            return pd.DataFrame(columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n\n    def _polling_loop(self):\n        """\n        Polling loop to fetch data and trigger callbacks.\n        """\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop started.")\n        while self._running:\n            for symbol in self.symbols:\n                df = self._fetch_ohlcv(symbol)\n                if not df.empty:\n                    prev_df = self.latest_data.get(symbol)\n                    # Only trigger update if new data is available\n                    if prev_df is None or not df.equals(prev_df):\n                        self.latest_data[symbol] = df\n                        for cb in self.callbacks:\n                            try:\n                                cb(symbol)\n                            except Exception as e:\n                                self.logger.error(f"Error in data update callback for {symbol}: {e}")\n            time.sleep(self.poll_interval)\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")\n\n    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):\n        """\n        Start the data feed polling loop in a background thread.\n        Optionally set the exchange client.\n        """\n        if exchange:\n            self.set_exchange(exchange)\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client must be set before starting DataFeed.")\n        if self._running:\n            self.logger.warning("DataFeed already running.")\n            return\n        self._running = True\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)\n        self._thread.start()\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the data feed polling loop.\n        """\n        self._running = False\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join()\n            self._thread = None\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed stopped.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. content:\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional\nimport threading\nimport time\n\nclass Monitor:\n    """\n    Monitor class for logging trades, sending alerts, and reporting status.\n    Includes log_trade, alert, and get_status methods as per system design.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("Monitor")\n        self._trade_log: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        self._alerts: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        self._status: Dict[str, Any] = {\n            "running": True,\n            "last_trade": None,\n            "last_alert": None,\n            "start_time": time.time(),\n            "trade_count": 0,\n            "alert_count": 0,\n        }\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    def log_trade(self, trade: Dict[str, Any]):\n        """\n        Log a trade event.\n        :param trade: Trade information dictionary.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            self._trade_log.append(trade)\n            self._status["last_trade"] = trade\n            self._status["trade_count"] += 1\n        self.logger.info(f"Trade logged: {trade}")\n\n    def alert(self, event: str):\n        """\n        Log an alert event.\n        :param event: Alert message string.\n        """\n        alert_info = {\n            "timestamp": time.time(),\n            "event": event\n        }\n        with self._lock:\n            self._alerts.append(alert_info)\n            self._status["last_alert"] = alert_info\n            self._status["alert_count"] += 1\n        self.logger.warning(f"ALERT: {event}")\n\n    def get_status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Get current bot status.\n        :return: Status dictionary.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            status_copy = self._status.copy()\n            status_copy["uptime_sec"] = time.time() - self._status["start_time"]\n        return status_copy\n\n    def get_logs(self) -> Default initial balance for backtest\n        self.final_balance: float = self.initial_balance\n        self.max_drawdown: float = 0.0\n        self.total_return: float = 0.0\n        self.win_rate: float = 0.0\n        self.total_trades: int = 0\n        self.profitable_trades: int = 0\n\n    def run(self, strategy: TrendStrategy, data: pd.DataFrame, risk: RiskManager) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Run backtest for the given strategy and data, using the provided risk manager.\n        :param strategy: TrendStrategy instance\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data\n        :param risk: RiskManager instance\n        :return: Results dictionary\n        """\n        balance = self.initial_balance\n        position = None\n        entry_price = 0.0\n        position_side = None\n        position_size = 0.0\n        self.trades = []\n        self.equity_curve = [balance]\n        self.max_drawdown = 0.0\n        self.total_trades = 0\n        self.profitable_trades = 0\n\n        for i in range(1, len(data)):\n            window = data.iloc[:i+1]\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(window)\n            price = window["close"].iloc[-1]\n            timestamp = window["timestamp"].iloc[-1] if "timestamp" in window.columns else i\n\n            # Simulate position management\n            if position is None and signal in ["long", "short"]:\n                # Open new position\n                position = {\n                    "side": signal,\n                    "entryPrice": price,\n                    "markPrice": price,\n                    "contracts": 1,\n                    "timestamp": timestamp\n                }\n                entry_price = price\n                position_side = signal\n                position_size = 1\n                self.total_trades += 1\n            elif position is not None:\n                # Update mark price\n                position["markPrice"] = price\n\n                # Check for stop-loss/take-profit\n                stop_loss_triggered = risk.apply_stop_loss(position)\n                take_profit_triggered = risk.apply_take_profit(position)\n\n                # Close position if triggered or opposite signal\n                close_position = False\n                reason = ""\n                if stop_loss_triggered:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "stop_loss"\n                elif take_profit_triggered:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "take_profit"\n                elif signal != position_side and signal in ["long", "short"]:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "signal_flip"\n\n                if close_position:\n                    # Calculate PnL\n                    pnl = 0.0\n                    if position_side == "long":\n                        pnl = (price - entry_price) * position_size\n                    elif position_side == "short":\n                        pnl = (entry_price - price) * position_size\n                    balance += pnl\n                    self.trades.append({\n                        "entry_price": entry_price,\n                        "exit_price": price,\n                        "side": position_side,\n                        "pnl": pnl,\n                        "reason": reason,\n                        "entry_time": position["timestamp"],\n                        "exit_time": timestamp\n                    })\n                    if pnl > 0:\n                        self.profitable_trades += 1\n                    position = None\n                    entry_price = 0.0\n                    position_side = None\n                    position_size = 0.0\n\n            self.equity_curve.append(balance)\n            # Update max drawdown\n            peak = max(self.equity_curve)\n            drawdown = (peak - balance) / peak if peak > 0 else 0\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.max_drawdown = drawdown\n\n        self.final_balance = balance\n        self.total_return = (self.final_balance - self.initial_balance) / self.initial_balance\n        self.win_rate = (self.profitable_trades / self.total_trades) if self.total_trades > 0 else 0.0\n\n        self.results = {\n            "initial_balance": self.initial_balance,\n            "final_balance": self.final_balance,\n            "total_return": self.total_return,\n            "max_drawdown": self.max_drawdown,\n            "total_trades": self.total_trades,\n            "win_rate": self.win_rate,\n            "trades": self.trades,\n            "equity_curve": self.equity_curve\n        }\n        return self.results\n\n    def report(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Return the backtest results.\n        :return: Results dictionary\n        """\n        return self.results\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n   data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n Uses polling via ccxt for CoinDCX Futures.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, poll_interval: float = 2.0):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DataFeed")\n        self.symbols: List[str] = []\n        self.callbacks: List[Callable[[str], None]] = []\n        self.latest_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame] = {}\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self._running = False\n        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None\n        self._exchange: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None\n\n    def subscribe(self, symbols: List[str]):\n        """\n        Subscribe to a list of trading symbols.\n        """\n        self.symbols = list(set(self.symbols) | set(symbols))\n        self.logger.info(f"Subscribed to symbols: {self.symbols}")\n\n    def set_exchange(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx):\n        """\n        Set the ccxt exchange client for data fetching.\n        """\n        self._exchange = exchange\n\n    def get_latest(self, symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Get the latest OHLCV data for a symbol.\n        """\n        if symbol in self.latest_data:\n            return self.latest_data[symbol]\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f"No data available for symbol: {symbol}")\n\n    def on_update(self, callback: Callable[[str], None]):\n        """\n        Register a callback to be called when new data is available for a symbol.\n        The callback receives the symbol as argument.\n        """\n        self.callbacks.append(callback)\n\n    def _fetch_ohlcv(self, symbol: str, timeframe: str = "1m", limit: int = 100) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Fetch OHLCV data for a symbol using ccxt.\n        """\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client not set in DataFeed.")\n        try:\n            ohlcv = self._exchange.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)\n            df = pd.DataFrame(ohlcv, columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n            df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"], unit="ms")\n            return df\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching OHLCV for {symbol}: {e}")\n            return pd.DataFrame(columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n\n    def _polling_loop(self):\n        """\n        Polling loop to fetch data and self._fetch_ohlcv(symbol)\n                if not df.empty:\n                    prev_df = self.latest_data.get(symbol)\n                    # Only trigger update if new data is available\n                    if prev_df is None or not df.equals(prev_df):\n                        self.latest_data[symbol] = df\n                        for cb in self.callbacks:\n                            try:\n                                cb(symbol)\n                            except Exception as e:\n                                self.logger.error(f"Error in data update callback for {symbol}: {e}")\n            time.sleep(self.poll_interval)\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")\n\n    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):\n        """\n        Start the data feed polling loop in a background thread.\n        Optionally set the exchange client.\n        """\n        if exchange:\n            self.set_exchange(exchange)\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client must be set before starting DataFeed.")\n        if self._running:\n            self.logger.warning("DataFeed already running.")\n            return\n        self._running = True\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)\n        self._thread.start()\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the data feed polling loop.\n        """\n        self._running = False\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join()\n            self._thread = None\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed stopped.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Default initial balance for backtest\n        self.final_balance: float = self.initial_balance\n        self.max_drawdown: float = 0.0\n        self.total_return: float = 0.0\n        self.win_rate: float = 0.0\n        self.total_trades: int = 0\n        self.profitable_trades: int = 0\n\n    def run(self, strategy: TrendStrategy, data: pd.DataFrame, risk: RiskManager) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Run backtest for the given strategy and data, using the provided risk manager.\n        :param strategy: TrendStrategy instance\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data\n        :param risk: RiskManager instance\n        :return: Results dictionary\n        """\n        balance = self.initial_balance\n        position = None\n        entry_price = 0.0\n        position_side = None\n        position_size = 0.0\n        self.trades = []\n        self.equity_curve = [balance]\n        self.max_drawdown = 0.0\n        self.total_trades = 0\n        self.profitable_trades = 0\n\n        for i in range(1, len(data)):\n            window = data.iloc[:i+1]\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(window)\n            price = window["close"].iloc[-1]\n            timestamp = window["timestamp"].iloc[-1] if "timestamp" in window.columns else i\n\n            # Simulate position management\n            if position is None and signal in ["long", "short"]:\n                # Open new position\n                position = {\n                    "side": signal,\n                    "entryPrice": price,\n                    "markPrice": price,\n                    "contracts": 1,\n                    "timestamp": timestamp\n                }\n                entry_price = price\n                position_side = signal\n                position_size = 1\n                self.total_trades += 1\n            elif position is not None:\n                # Update mark price\n                position["markPrice"] = price\n\n                # Check for stop-loss/take-profit\n                stop_loss_triggered = risk.apply_stop_loss(position)\n                take_profit_triggered = risk.apply_take_profit(position)\n\n                # Close position if triggered or opposite signal\n                close_position = False\n                reason = ""\n                if stop_loss_triggered:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "stop_loss"\n                elif take_profit_triggered:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "take_profit"\n                elif signal != position_side and signal in ["long", "short"]:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "signal_flip"\n\n                if close_position:\n                    # Calculate PnL\n                    pnl = 0.0\n                    if position_side == "long":\n                        pnl = (price - entry_price) * position_size\n                    elif position_side == "short":\n                        pnl = (entry_price - price) * position_size\n                    balance += pnl\n                    self.trades.append({\n                        "entry_price": entry_price,\n                        "exit_price": price,\n                        "side": position_side,\n                        "pnl": pnl,\n                        "reason": reason,\n                        "entry_time": position["timestamp"],\n                        "exit_time": timestamp\n                    })\n                    if pnl > 0:\n                        self.profitable_trades += 1\n                    position = None\n                    entry_price = 0.0\n                    position_side = None\n                    position_size = 0.0\n\n            self.equity_curve.append(balance)\n            # Update max drawdown\n            peak = max(self.equity_curve)\n            drawdown = (peak - balance) / peak if peak > 0 else 0\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.max_drawdown = drawdown\n\n        self.final_balance = balance\n        self.total_return = (self.final_balance - self.initial_balance) / self.initial_balance\n        self.win_rate = (self.profitable_trades / self.total_trades) if self.total_trades > 0 else 0.0\n\n        self.results = {\n            "initial_balance": self.initial_balance,\n            "final_balance": self.final_balance,\n            "total_return": self.total_return,\n            "max_drawdown": self.max_drawdown,\n            "total_trades": self.total_trades,\n            "win_rate": self.win_rate,\n            "trades": self.trades,\n            "equity_curve": self.equity_curve\n        }\n        return self.results\n\n    def report(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Return the backtest results.\n        :return: Results dictionary\n        """\n        return self.results\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/custom_trading_bot/utils.py has been successfully created, with content:
import logging
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional, List
import pandas as pd

def utc_to_local(utc_dt: datetime, tz_offset: int = 0) -> datetime:
    """
    Convert UTC datetime to local time with given offset in hours.
    """
    return utc_dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc).astimezone(timezone.utc).astimezone(
        timezone(timedelta(hours=tz_offset))
    )

def timestamp_to_str(ts: float, fmt: str = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") -> str:
    """
    Convert a timestamp (seconds) to a formatted string.
    """
    return datetime.fromtimestamp(ts).strftime(fmt)

def ms_to_str(ms: int, fmt: str = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") -> str:
    """
    Convert a timestamp in milliseconds to a formatted string.
    """
    return datetime.fromtimestamp(ms / 1000.0).strftime(fmt)

def format_trade(trade: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    """
    Format a trade dictionary for logging or display.
    """
    entry_time = trade.get("entry_time")
    exit_time = trade.get("exit_time")
    entry_price = trade.get("entry_price")
    exit_price = trade.get("exit_price")
    side = trade.get("side")
    pnl = trade.get("pnl")
    reason = trade.get("reason", "")
    return (f"Trade [{side}] Entry: {entry_price} at {entry_time}, "
            f"Exit: {exit_price} at {exit_time}, PnL: {pnl:.2f}, Reason: {reason}")

def format_alert(alert: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    """
    Format an alert dictionary for logging or display.
    """
    ts = alert.get("timestamp", time.time())
    event = alert.get("event", "")
    return f"ALERT at {timestamp_to_str(ts)}: {event}"

def safe_get(d: Dict[str, Any], key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    """
    Safely get a value from a dictionary, returning default if not found.
    """
    return d[key] if key in d else default

def handle_exception(e: Exception, logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None, context: str = ""):
    """
    Handle exceptions by logging them.
    """
    msg = f"Exception in {context}: {str(e)}"
    if logger:
        logger.error(msg)
    else:
        print(msg)

def df_to_dict_list(df: pd.DataFrame) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    Convert a pandas DataFrame to a list of dictionaries.
    """
    return df.to_dict(orient="records")

def dict_list_to_df(dict_list: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Convert a list of dictionaries to a pandas DataFrame.
    """
    return pd.DataFrame(dict_list)

def round_float(val: Any, digits: int = 4) -> float:
    """
    Safely round a float value.
    """
    try:
        return round(float(val), digits)
    except Exception:
        return 0.0

def validate_symbol(symbol: str) -> bool:
    """
    Validate trading symbol format (e.g., 'BTC/USDT').
    """
    return isinstance(symbol, str) and "/" in symbol and len(symbol.split("/")) == 2

def retry_on_exception(func, retries: int = 3, delay: float = 1.0, logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None):
    """
    Retry a function on exception up to a number of times.
    """
    for attempt in range(retries):
        try:
            return func()
        except Exception as e:
            if logger:
                logger.warning(f"Retry {attempt+1}/{retries} failed: {e}")
            time.sleep(delay)
    if logger:
        logger.error(f"All {retries} retries failed for function {func.__name__}")
    return None

Initialize Exchange\n    exchange = Exchange()\n    try:\n        exchange.connect(config.api_key, config.api_secret)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Failed to connect to exchange: {e}")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Parameters for trend-following strategy")\n    risk_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Risk management parameters")\n    assets: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="List of trading symbols")\n    notification_settings: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Notification preferences")\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n        with open(path, "rb") as f:\n            data = f.read()\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.decrypt(data)\n        else:\n            data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n            raise ValueError(f"Invalid config file: {e}")\n\n    def save(self, path: str, encryption_key: str = None):\n        config_dict = self.dict()\n        data = json.dumps(config_dict, indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Optional[Dict[str, Any]], signal: str) -> bool:\n        """\n        Check if a new trade is allowed based on risk parameters.\n        :param position: Current position dict for the symbol (can be None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position   if position_size >= self.max_position_size:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Position size {position_size} exceeds max {self.max_position_size}.")\n            return False\n\n        # Check 0\n        if entry_value > 0:\n            drawdown = -unrealized_pnl / entry_value\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.logger.warning(f"Drawdown {drawdown:.2%} exceeds max {self.max_drawdown:.2%}.")\n                return False\n\n        self.logger.info("Risk check passed, trade allowed.")\n        return True\n\n    def apply_stop_loss(self, position: "").lower()\n        if entry_price == 0 or current_price == 0 or side not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Insufficient data for stop-loss check.")\n            return False\n\n self.logger.info(f"Stop-loss triggered for {side} position at price {current_price}.")\n        return stop_loss_triggered\n\n    def apply_take_profit(self, position: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n Uses polling via ccxt for CoinDCX Futures.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, poll_interval: float = 2.0):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DataFeed")\n        self.symbols: List[str] = []\n        self.callbacks: List[Callable[[str], None]] = []\n        self.latest_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame] = {}\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self._running = False\n        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None\n        self._exchange: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None\n\n    def subscribe(self, symbols: List[str]):\n        """\n        Subscribe to a list of trading symbols.\n        """\n        self.symbols = list(set(self.symbols) | set(symbols))\n        self.logger.info(f"Subscribed to symbols: {self.symbols}")\n\n    def set_exchange(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx):\n        """\n        Set the ccxt exchange client for data fetching.\n        """\n        self._exchange = exchange\n\n    def get_latest(self, symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Get the latest OHLCV data for a symbol.\n        """\n        if symbol in self.latest_data:\n            return self.latest_data[symbol]\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f"No data available for symbol: {symbol}")\n\n    def on_update(self, callback: Callable[[str], None]):\n        """\n        Register a callback to be called when new data is available for a symbol.\n        The callback receives the symbol as argument.\n        """\n        self.callbacks.append(callback)\n\n    def _fetch_ohlcv(self, symbol: str, timeframe: str = "1m", limit: int = 100) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        """\n        Fetch OHLCV data for a symbol using ccxt.\n        """\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client not set in DataFeed.")\n        try:\n            ohlcv = self._exchange.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)\n            df = pd.DataFrame(ohlcv, columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n            df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"], unit="ms")\n            return df\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching OHLCV for {symbol}: {e}")\n            return pd.DataFrame(columns=["timestamp", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"])\n\n    def _polling_loop(self):\n        """\n        Polling loop to fetch data and trigger callbacks.\n        """\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop started.")\n        while self._running:\n            for symbol in self.symbols:\n                df = self._fetch_ohlcv(symbol)\n                if not df.empty:\n                    prev_df = self.latest_data.get(symbol)\n                    # Only trigger update if new data is available\n                    if prev_df is None or not df.equals(prev_df):\n                        self.latest_data[symbol] = df\n                        for cb in self.callbacks:\n                            try:\n                                cb(symbol)\n                            except Exception as e:\n                                self.logger.error(f"Error in data update callback for {symbol}: {e}")\n            time.sleep(self.poll_interval)\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed polling loop stopped.")\n\n    def start(self, exchange: ccxt.coincdx = None):\n        """\n        Start the data feed polling loop in a background thread.\n        Optionally set the exchange client.\n        """\n        if exchange:\n            self.set_exchange(exchange)\n        if not self._exchange:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange client must be set before starting DataFeed.")\n        if self._running:\n            self.logger.warning("DataFeed already running.")\n            return\n        self._running = True\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._polling_loop, daemon=True)\n        self._thread.start()\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed started.")\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the data feed polling loop.\n        """\n        self._running = False\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join()\n            self._thread = None\n        self.logger.info("DataFeed stopped.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        self._alerts: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        self._status: Dict[str, Any] = {\n            "running": True,\n            "last_trade": None,\n            "last_alert": None,\n            "start_time": time.time(),\n            "trade_count": 0,\n            "alert_count": 0,\n        }\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    def log_trade(self, server.starttls()\n                server.login(smtp_user, smtp_password)\n                server.sendmail(from_addr, [to_addr], message.as_string())\n            self.logger.info(f"Email sent to Default initial balance for backtest\n        self.final_balance: float = self.initial_balance\n        self.max_drawdown: float = 0.0\n        self.total_return: float = 0.0\n        self.win_rate: float = 0.0\n        self.total_trades: int = 0\n        self.profitable_trades: int = 0\n\n    def run(self, strategy: TrendStrategy, data: pd.DataFrame, risk: RiskManager) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Run backtest for the given strategy and data, using the provided risk manager.\n        :param strategy: TrendStrategy instance\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data\n        :param risk: RiskManager instance\n        :return: Results dictionary\n        """\n        balance = self.initial_balance\n        position = None\n        entry_price = 0.0\n        position_side = None\n        position_size = 0.0\n        self.trades = []\n        self.equity_curve = [balance]\n        self.max_drawdown = 0.0\n        self.total_trades = 0\n        self.profitable_trades = 0\n\n        for i in range(1, len(data)):\n            window = data.iloc[:i+1]\n            signal = strategy.generate_signal(window)\n            price = window["close"].iloc[-1]\n            timestamp = window["timestamp"].iloc[-1] if "timestamp" in window.columns else i\n\n            # Simulate position management\n            if position is None and signal in ["long", "short"]:\n                # Open new position\n                position = {\n                    "side": signal,\n                    "entryPrice": price,\n                    "markPrice": price,\n                    "contracts": 1,\n                    "timestamp": timestamp\n                }\n                entry_price = price\n                position_side = signal\n                position_size = 1\n                self.total_trades += 1\n            elif position is not None:\n                # Update mark price\n                position["markPrice"] = price\n\n                # Check for stop-loss/take-profit\n                stop_loss_triggered = risk.apply_stop_loss(position)\n                take_profit_triggered = risk.apply_take_profit(position)\n\n                # Close position if triggered or opposite signal\n                close_position = False\n                reason = ""\n                if stop_loss_triggered:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "stop_loss"\n                elif take_profit_triggered:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "take_profit"\n                elif signal != position_side and signal in ["long", "short"]:\n                    close_position = True\n                    reason = "signal_flip"\n\n                if close_position:\n                    # Calculate PnL\n                    pnl = 0.0\n                    if position_side == "long":\n                        pnl = (price - entry_price) * position_size\n                    elif position_side == "short":\n                        pnl = (entry_price - price) * position_size\n                    balance += pnl\n                    self.trades.append({\n                        "entry_price": entry_price,\n                        "exit_price": price,\n                        "side": position_side,\n                        "pnl": pnl,\n                        "reason": reason,\n                        "entry_time": position["timestamp"],\n                        "exit_time": timestamp\n                    })\n                    if pnl > 0:\n                        self.profitable_trades += 1\n                    position = None\n                    entry_price = 0.0\n                    position_side = None\n                    position_size = 0.0\n\n            self.equity_curve.append(balance)\n            # Update max drawdown\n            peak = max(self.equity_curve)\n            drawdown = (peak - balance) / peak if peak > 0 else 0\n            if drawdown > self.max_drawdown:\n                self.max_drawdown = drawdown\n\n        self.final_balance = balance\n        self.total_return = (self.final_balance - self.initial_balance) / self.initial_balance\n        self.win_rate = (self.profitable_trades / self.total_trades) if self.total_trades > 0 else 0.0\n\n        self.results = {\n            "initial_balance": self.initial_balance,\n            "final_balance": self.final_balance,\n            "total_return": self.total_return,\n            "max_drawdown": self.max_drawdown,\n            "total_trades": self.total_trades,\n            "win_rate": self.win_rate,\n            "trades": self.trades,\n            "equity_curve": self.equity_curve\n        }\n        return self.results\n\n    def report(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Return the backtest results.\n        :return: Results dictionary\n        """\n        return self.results\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. """\n    Retry a function on exception up to a number of times.\n    """\n    for attempt in range(retries):\n        try:\n            return func()\n        except Exception as e:\n            if Determine order parameters\n                side = "buy" if signal == "long" else "sell"\n                qty = config.strategy_params.get("position_size", 1.0)\n                price = data.iloc[-1]["close"]\n                order_type = config.strategy_params.get("order_type", "market")\n                order = exchange.place_order(symbol, side, qty, price, order_type)\n                monitor.log_trade(order)\n                notifier.send_push(f"Trade executed: {order}")\n            else:\n                monitor.alert(f"Risk check failed for {symbol} with signal {signal}")\n                notifier.send_push(f"Risk event: {symbol} signal {signal} blocked by risk manager")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f"Error in data update for {symbol}: {e}")\n\n    # Register callback for data updates\n    data_feed.on_update(on_data_update)\n\n    # Main event loop\n    logger.info("Custom Trading Bot started.")\n    try:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logger.info("Bot stopped by user.")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f"Bot encountered an error: {e}")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. BotConfig(BaseModel):\n    api_key: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API key")\n    api_secret: str = Field(..., description="CoinDCX API secret")\n    strategy_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Parameters for trend-following strategy")\n    risk_params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Risk management parameters")\n    assets: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="List of trading symbols")\n    notification_settings: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Notification preferences")\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str, encryption_key: str = None) -> "BotConfig":\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Config file not found: {path}")\n        with open(path, "rb") as f:\n            data = f.read()\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.decrypt(data)\n        else:\n            data = data.decode("utf-8")\n        try:\n            config_dict = json.loads(data)\n            return cls.parse_obj(config_dict)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:\n            raise ValueError(f"Invalid config file: {e}")\n\n    def save(self, path: str, encryption_key: str = None):\n        config_dict = self.dict()\n        data = json.dumps(config_dict, indent=2)\n        if encryption_key:\n            fernet = Fernet(encryption_key)\n            data = fernet.encrypt(data.encode("utf-8"))\n            with open(path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n        else:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(data)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def generate_encryption_key() -> str:\n        return Optional\n\nclass Exchange:\n    """\n    Exchange class for CoinDCX Futures integration using ccxt.\n    Supports connect, get_balance, fetch_ticker, place_order, cancel_order, and get_positions.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.api_key: Optional[str] = None\n        self.api_secret: Optional[str] = None\n        self.client: Optional[ccxt.coincdx] = None\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("Exchange")\n\n    def connect(self, api_key: str, api_secret: str):\n        """\n        Connect to CoinDCX Futures using provided API credentials.\n        """\n        self.api_key = api_key\n        self.api_secret = api_secret\n        try:\n            self.client = ccxt.coincdx({\n                \'apiKey\': self.api_key,\n                \'secret\': self.api_secret,\n                \'enableRateLimit\': True,\n                \'options\': {\n                    \'defaultType\': \'future\'\n                }\n            })\n            # Test self.logger.error(f"Failed to connect to CoinDCX Futures: {e}")\n            raise\n\n    def get_balance(self) -> Dict:\n        """\n        Get account balance.\n        """\n        if not self.client:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange not connected.")\n        try:\n            balance = self.client.fetch_balance()\n            self.logger.info("Fetched account balance.")\n            return balance\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching balance: {e}")\n            raise\n\n    def fetch_ticker(self, symbol: str) -> Dict:\n        """\n        Fetch ticker data for a symbol.\n        """\n        if not self.client:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange not connected.")\n        try:\n            ticker = self.client.fetch_ticker(symbol)\n            self.logger.info(f"Fetched ticker for {symbol}.")\n            return ticker\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching ticker for {symbol}: {e}")\n            raise\n\n    def place_order(self, symbol: str, side: str, qty: float, price: float, type_: str) -> Dict:\n        """\n        Place an order on CoinDCX Futures.\n        :param symbol: Trading symbol (e.g., \'BTC/USDT\')\n        :param side: \'buy\' or \'sell\'\n        :param qty: Quantity to trade\n        :param price: Price for limit orders; ignored for market orders\n        :param type_: \'market\' or \'limit\'\n        :return: Order response dict\n        """\n        if not self.client:\n            raise RuntimeError("Exchange not connected.")\n        try:\n            order_type = type_.lower()\n            params = {}\n            if order_type == "market":\n                order = self.client.create_order(\n                    symbol=symbol,\n                    type="market",\n                    side=side,\n                    amount=qty,\n                    params=params\n                )\n            elif order_type == "limit":\n                order = self.client.create_order(\n                    symbol=symbol,\n                    type="limit",\n                    side=side,\n                    amount=qty,\n                    price=price,\n                    params=params\n                )\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported order type: {type_}")\n            self.logger.info(f"Placed {order_type} order: {order}")\n            return order\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error placing order for {symbol}: {e}")\n            raise\n\n    def cancel_order(self, order_id: str, symbol: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict:\n        """\n        Cancel an order by order_id.\n        :param order_id: The order ID to cancel\n        :param symbol: Trading symbol (required by some exchanges)\n        :return: Cancel response dict\n        """\n        if not self.client:\n            raise Default to EMA\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", {})\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", {"buy": 0, "sell": 0})\n        self.last_signal: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def update_params(self, params: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.params.update(params)\n        self.indicator = self.params.get("indicator", self.indicator)\n        self.indicator_params = self.params.get("indicator_params", self.indicator_params)\n        self.signal_thresholds = self.params.get("signal_thresholds", self.signal_thresholds)\n\n    def generate_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        """\n        Generate trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\') based on selected trend indicator.\n        :param data: pd.DataFrame with OHLCV data, must contain \'close\' column.\n        :return: Signal string\n        """\n        if data is None or len(data) < 2 or "close" not in data.columns:\n            return "neutral"\n\n        if self.indicator == "EMA":\n            return self._ema_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "SMA":\n            return self._sma_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "MACD":\n            return self._macd_signal(data)\n        elif self.indicator == "RSI":\n            return self._rsi_signal(data)\n        else:\n            # Default fallback\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _ema_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        ema = data["close"].ewm(span=period, adjust=False).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_ema = ema.iloc[-2]\n        curr_ema = ema.iloc[-1]\n        # Signal: price crosses above EMA -> long, below -> short\n        if price > curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_ema:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_ema and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_ema:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _sma_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 20))\n        sma = data["close"].rolling(window=period).mean()\n        price = data["close"].iloc[-1]\n        prev_sma = sma.iloc[-2]\n        curr_sma = sma.iloc[-1]\n        if price > curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] <= prev_sma:\n            return "long"\n        elif price < curr_sma and data["close"].iloc[-2] >= prev_sma:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _macd_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        fast_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("fast_period", 12))\n        slow_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("slow_period", 26))\n        signal_period = int(self.indicator_params.get("signal_period", 9))\n        close = data["close"]\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal = macd.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        prev_macd = macd.iloc[-2] - signal.iloc[-2]\n        curr_macd = macd.iloc[-1] - signal.iloc[-1]\n        # MACD crosses above signal -> long, below -> short\n        if prev_macd < 0 and curr_macd > 0:\n            return "long"\n        elif prev_macd > 0 and curr_macd < 0:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\n    def _rsi_signal(self, data: pd.DataFrame) -> str:\n        period = int(self.indicator_params.get("period", 14))\n        close = data["close"]\n        delta = close.diff()\n        gain = (delta.where(delta > 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        rs = gain / (loss + 1e-9)\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n        curr_rsi = rsi.iloc[-1]\n        buy_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("buy", 30))\n        sell_th = float(self.signal_thresholds.get("sell", 70))\n        if curr_rsi < buy_th:\n            return "long"\n        elif curr_rsi > sell_th:\n            return "short"\n        else:\n            return "neutral"\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. None if no position)\n        :param signal: Trading signal (\'long\', \'short\', \'neutral\')\n        :return: True if trade is allowed, False otherwise\n        """\n        # If no signal, do not trade\n        if signal not in ["long", "short"]:\n            self.logger.info("Signal is neutral, no trade.")\n            return False\n\n        # If no position, allow trade\n        if position
